title: Neomammillaria mammillaris
taxonomy: Neomammillaria mammillaris is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Mammillaria, which is a group of small to medium-sized cacti. The genus Mammillaria is further classified into several subgenera, with Mammillaria subgenus Mammillaria being the most widely accepted classification. Mammillaria subgenus Mammillaria includes species like Mammillaria elongata and Mammillaria geminispina, which are closely related to Neomammillaria mammillaris. The exact classification of Neomammillaria mammillaris is still a topic of debate among botanists.

intro: Neomammillaria mammillaris, also known as the Pincushion Cactus, is a type of succulent plant native to Mexico, characterized by its dense, globular stems and vibrant pink to red flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including wounds, infections, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria mammillaris include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. In herbal medicine, Neomammillaria mammillaris is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
